name: ascending-order
description: Due callbacks fire in ascending atUt order regardless of schedule/insertion order.

name: inclusive-boundary
description: A callback scheduled exactly at the target UT fires (atUt <= ut is inclusive).

name: not-yet-due-is-held
description: advanceTo only fires callbacks with atUt <= target; later ones stay pending until a later advance.

name: future-schedule-not-fired
description: A callback scheduled after the target UT never fires on that advance.

name: cancel-before-due
description: A cancelled callback never fires even once its atUt is reached.

name: backward-advance-is-strict-noop
description: advanceTo to a UT strictly before currentUt is a full no-op: now() doesn't move and nothing fires (strict '<' guard, not '<=').

name: same-ut-reschedule-drains
description: A callback that schedules another callback at the SAME ut it just fired at is drained within the same advanceTo (re-entrancy-safe drain), not stranded for a later advance.

name: reentrant-future-due-drains-in-order
description: A callback that re-entrantly schedules another callback at a still-due future UT (<= target) fires it within the SAME advanceTo, in ascending-atUt order relative to anything else pending.

name: repeat-advance-to-same-ut-still-fires-new-schedule
description: advanceTo(ut) is idempotent w.r.t. now() but NOT a no-op for freshly scheduled callbacks: scheduling at the current ut and calling advanceTo(ut) again still fires it.

name: chained-reentrant-drain-multiple-levels
description: Re-entrancy drains through more than one level: a fires, schedules b at <= ut, b fires and schedules c at <= ut, all within one advanceTo, plus an unrelated already-pending callback interleaves by atUt order.
